drone.m1_easy_desc: Simple delivery route — great for beginners!
drone.m1_med_desc: Program a drone to deliver medical supplies to an oil platform. Avoid obstacles.
drone.m1_hard_desc: Multiple drones, dynamic obstacles, and tight battery limits. For expert programmers.
drone.m2_easy_desc: Basic city grid navigation — learn the patterns.
drone.m2_med_desc: Optimize a drone delivery route across Riyadh to minimize time and battery use.
drone.m2_hard_desc: Rush hour in Riyadh — 7 packages, moving obstacles, 60% battery limit.
drone.addBlocksHint: Click blocks above to build your program...
drone.tip: Pick up the package, navigate to the target and drop it. Fewer blocks = higher score!
drone.crashed: Crashed into obstacle! Reset and try again.

robot.m1_easy_desc: Build a basic robot for simple pipeline tasks.
robot.m1_med_desc: Build a robot to work on an oil pipeline assembly line within a tight budget.
robot.m1_hard_desc: Critical pipeline repair — extremely tight budget, maximum stats required.
robot.m2_easy_desc: Design a simple search robot for open terrain.
robot.m2_med_desc: Design a robot for search-and-rescue operations in a desert environment.
robot.m2_hard_desc: Night rescue in collapsed ruins — all stats must be maxed within budget.

entrepreneur.m1_easy_desc: Simple startup decisions — learn the basics of entrepreneurship.
entrepreneur.m1_med_desc: Launch an educational technology startup for Saudi schools. Choose your team and strategy.
entrepreneur.m1_hard_desc: High-stakes launch — investor pressure, regulatory hurdles, 4 critical decisions.
entrepreneur.m2_med_desc: Your startup has traction. Decide whether to scale locally or expand to BRICS+ markets.
entrepreneur.m2_hard_desc: Crisis mode — competitor threat, talent flight, and expansion decision all at once.
entrepreneur.tip: There is no single right answer — your choices reflect your leadership style.
entrepreneur.m1s1_context: You are launching a tech startup in a BRICS city. Which market will you target?
entrepreneur.m1s1_fb1: Saudi Vision 2030 is driving rapid tech adoption. Great growth potential!
entrepreneur.m1s1_fb2: Strong engineering talent and growing startup ecosystem. Solid choice!
entrepreneur.m1s1_fb3: Enormous market. Scale wins here, but margins are tight.
entrepreneur.m1s2_context: Your MVP is ready. How will you fund growth?
entrepreneur.m1s2_fb1: Smart money brings mentorship and connections. Worth the equity.
entrepreneur.m1s2_fb2: Debt keeps you in control but adds monthly pressure.
entrepreneur.m1s2_fb3: Hardest path but you keep 100% ownership. Discipline wins.
entrepreneur.m1s3_context: Business is growing. Time to hire. What team structure?
entrepreneur.m1s3_fb1: Quality over quantity. High output but high burn rate.
entrepreneur.m1s3_fb2: More hands on deck. Lower cost per head but needs strong management.
entrepreneur.m1s3_fb3: Access to global talent and time zones. Communication is the challenge.
entrepreneur.m1s4_context: A major competitor just launched a free alternative. Your paid users are leaving. What now?
entrepreneur.m1s4_opt1: Go premium — double down on enterprise features
entrepreneur.m1s4_opt2: Pivot to B2B — sell to schools directly
entrepreneur.m1s4_fb1: Enterprise customers pay more and churn less. Smart positioning.
entrepreneur.m1s4_fb2: B2B contracts provide stable revenue. Schools need specialized tools.
entrepreneur.m1s4_fb3: Risky but could recapture market share fast.
entrepreneur.m2s1_context: Your startup hit $50K monthly revenue. Time to expand. Where?
entrepreneur.m2s1_fb1: Familiar territory. Lower risk, faster time to market.
entrepreneur.m2s1_fb2: Booming tech ecosystem. High competition but massive opportunity.
entrepreneur.m2s1_fb3: First-mover advantage in many sectors. Infrastructure is the challenge.
entrepreneur.m2s2_context: The new market needs a localized product. What will you build?
entrepreneur.m2s2_fb1: Physical products create moats but need supply chains.
entrepreneur.m2s2_fb2: Software scales globally with low marginal cost. Classic SaaS play.
entrepreneur.m2s2_fb3: High-touch, high-margin. Builds deep relationships but hard to scale.
entrepreneur.m2s3_context: You need a country manager for the new market. Who do you hire?
entrepreneur.m2s3_opt1: Local executive — knows the market deeply
entrepreneur.m2s3_opt2: Internal transfer — knows your culture and product
entrepreneur.m2s3_opt3: Fresh MBA — hungry, data-driven, lower cost
entrepreneur.m2s3_fb1: Local knowledge is invaluable. They speak the language of the market.
entrepreneur.m2s3_fb2: Culture fit matters. They can translate your vision to the new team.
entrepreneur.m2s3_fb3: Fresh perspective and energy. May need more mentoring though.
entrepreneur.m2s4_context: A well-funded competitor is copying your product and offering it for free. Your team morale is dropping.
entrepreneur.m2s4_opt1: Acquire them — if you cannot beat them, buy them
entrepreneur.m2s4_opt2: Differentiate — build features they cannot copy easily
entrepreneur.m2s4_fb1: Bold move. Removes the threat and gains their team.
entrepreneur.m2s4_fb2: Innovation wins long-term. Deep tech moats are hard to replicate.
entrepreneur.m2s4_fb3: Turning enemies into allies. Creative strategy.
entrepreneur.shock_regulation: New government regulation requires compliance within 30 days. Costs increase by 20%.
entrepreneur.shock_viral: Your product went viral! User signups tripled overnight but servers are struggling.
entrepreneur.shock_currency: Currency devaluation in your market. Import costs jump 30%.
entrepreneur.shock_talent: Your lead developer got poached by a tech giant. Key knowledge is walking out the door.
